title: Evilutionary-Tree
subtitle: A flexible data project for exploring evolutionary paths and related information across science and fantasy settings.
description: Stores collections of real, fantasy, and other kinds of data in one place.
Designed to make evolutionary paths and related information easier to explore, compare, and understand across both science and fantasy settings.
features: Structured storage for real and fantasy data
Clear viewing of evolutionary paths and related information
Centralized data storage for mixed (real + fantasy) datasets
Search and filtering for fast data lookup
Expandable stat system with flexible schema
Designed for future database-driven queries instead of hardcoded logic
statusDetails: Transitioning from static storage to query-driven system
Improving data relationships and evolution linking
Planning scalable backend for future expansion

apiStructure: The project is currently centered around redesigning how data is stored, managed, and organized for future expansion.

progressNotes: Initial hardcoded stat system (Spring 2021 → Fall 2021)
Built first version using nested Python dictionaries.
Defined core stats: HP, MP, moves, range, attack, defense.
Organized entities by name and basic categories like gods and people.
Expansion of hardcoded system (Fall 2021 → Spring 2022)
Gradually added more entities and stat variations.
Improved organization of data structures.
Started seeing limitations of hardcoded approach.
Pre-migration restructuring (Spring 2022 → Early 2023)
Refined stat categories and naming consistency.
Prepared data for external storage.
Began planning for scalable data management.
Google Sheets migration + system expansion (2023)
Moved all stat storage to Google Sheets.
Downloaded data into Python as dict-based variables.
Added new systems: titles, classes, skills, and magic.
Replaced moves with SP (skill-based system).
Helper classes and early evolution system (2023 → Early 2024)
Created helper classes to wrap and access stat dictionaries.
Encapsulated data with getter-based access patterns.
Built first evolution system using linked multi-branch relationships.
JSON data system rewrite (July 2024 → Late 2024)
Reworked downloader to output JSON instead of Python files.
Decoupled data from code structure.
Updated helpers to retrieve data through callable references.
Improved internal data handling and flexibility.
Database architecture transition (Nov 2025 → Present)
Unified all data into a single structured Google Sheet.
Shifted from static storage to database-oriented design.
Planning full DB-backed system for dynamic queries.
Replacing hardcoded logic with query-driven data access.
